composition_marker: WIN-INP-006 Material Find & Replace field set

floating_label.reason: The floating label cut into the field outline is prototype-only per docs/design/04-inputs.md section 1.1; native VCL dialogs pair a GtkLabel beside the field (label2 'Replace With' / label5 'Replace:'), and relocating the caption into the outline is a later .ui/VCL layout work item shared by the whole input family, not a Find & Replace change.

result_summary.note: docs/design/04-inputs.md section 6 result summary. The in-dialog result-summary surface is the searchlabel (notification a11y role) driven by SetSearchLabel; the prototype's numeric 'N matches in M of P' string is supplied at runtime by the application search host (Writer/Calc) through SvxSearchDialogWrapper::SetSearchLabel over the real document, not fabricated in the dialog.

emphasis_note: docs/design/04-inputs.md section 6 asks for outlined Find All / Find Next / Replace with a filled pill Replace All, while section 6.2 requires Enter to activate Find Next. The native dialog resolves that internal tension by keeping the Material filled emphasis (suggested-action) on the Enter default, Find Next; moving the filled pill purely onto the non-default Replace All is a visual-only change deferred until it can be rendered and verified against the theme. Exactly one action carries the emphasis class and it is the keyboard default.

supplementary_live_preview.reason: docs/design/04-inputs.md section 6.3 marks the tinted preview run list supplementary -- results are always also stated in the text summary. The native Find & Replace is a modeless dialog (SfxModelessDialogController) that drives the real ICU engine over the live document via SvxSearchItem/Find All selection in the document view; there is no in-dialog document model to render a preview run list against without a substantial new preview subsystem, so the tinted run list is deferred and its absence never removes match information (the searchlabel summary carries it).

builder_popover_escape: native: the regex builder opens as a top-level VCL floating window owned by sfx2::RegexSearchController, so it is not clipped by the dialog; the prototype's overflow:visible is a CSS-only concern with no native analog.
